skill.name: brevo-automation
skill.description: Automate Brevo (Sendinblue) tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): manage email campaigns, create/edit templates, track senders, and monitor campaign performance. Always search tools first for current sche...
skill.summary: Automate Brevo email marketing operations through Composio toolkit integration
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 107 potential external command execution patterns and network activity. Upon manual review, all findings are false positives from markdown documentation backticks used for tool names like BREVO_LIST_EMAIL_CAMPAIGNS and code fence examples. The network reference at line 22 is a legitimate documentation URL (https://rube.app/mcp) for the MCP server endpoint. No actual code execution or malicious activity present. This is pure documentation for a Brevo email marketing automation skill.

content.value_statement: Eliminate manual Brevo email marketing tasks. Automate campaign management, template creation, and sender verification through natural language commands with Claude.
actual_capabilities: List and filter email campaigns by status, type, and date range
Update campaign content, subject lines, and scheduling
Create and manage email templates with HTML content
Retrieve verified sender information for campaigns
Configure A/B testing settings for campaigns
Handle pagination for large campaign and template lists
limitations: Requires Rube MCP server connection and active Brevo authentication
Cannot send campaigns directly - must use Brevo web interface for final send
Sender verification must be completed through Brevo web interface
Rate limited by Brevo API account tier
Template deletion only works for inactive templates

use_cases.title: Email Marketing Manager
use_cases.description: Marketing professionals who need to manage multiple Brevo campaigns and templates without navigating the web interface manually.

use_cases.title: Campaign Operations Team
use_cases.description: Operations teams that need to bulk update campaign settings, review sender status, and manage template libraries at scale.

use_cases.title: Growth Marketers
use_cases.description: Growth marketers running A/B tests on email campaigns who need quick access to configure and review campaign performance settings.

prompt_templates.title: List Recent Campaigns
prompt_templates.prompt: List all sent email campaigns from the past 7 days. Show me the campaign names, status, and basic statistics.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick overview of recent campaign performance

prompt_templates.title: Update Campaign Subject
prompt_templates.prompt: Update campaign ID 12345 to change the subject line to 'New Summer Sale Starts Today'. Keep all other settings the same.

prompt_templates.title: Create Email Template
prompt_templates.prompt:
Create a new email template named 'Welcome Series' with subject 'Welcome to Our Community'. Use sender ID 5 and include this HTML content:

Welcome!
prompt_templates.scenario: Building a reusable template for future campaigns

prompt_templates.title: Configure A/B Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up A/B testing on campaign 67890. Test subject lines '50% Off Today' vs '25% Off Everything'. Split test at 30%, use click rate as winner criteria, and wait 24 hours before declaring winner.
prompt_templates.scenario: Setting up an A/B test campaign for optimization

output_examples.input: Show me all active email templates
output_examples.output: Found 12 active templates: Welcome Series (ID: 101), Newsletter Template (ID: 102), Promotional Banner (ID: 105), and 9 others. Use template IDs for updates or reference specific templates by name for detailed operations.
Retrieved 8 sent campaigns from January 1-31, 2025. Total recipients: 45,230. Average open rate: 24.5%. Campaigns include 'New Year Launch', 'Winter Sale', and 'Product Announcement'.
3 verified senders found: notifications@company.com (ID: 1), marketing@company.com (ID: 2), and support@company.com (ID: 3). All are ready for use in campaigns and templates.

best_practices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first before any Brevo operations to get current tool schemas and ensure connection is active
Use the excludeHtmlContent parameter when listing campaigns to reduce response size and improve performance
Verify sender status with GET_ALL_SENDERS before creating campaigns to avoid failures from unverified sender emails
Use template personalization syntax {{contact.ATTRIBUTE}} for dynamic content instead of hardcoded values
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to send campaigns directly through the API - use the Brevo web interface for final campaign sends
Avoid setting startDate without endDate when filtering campaigns - these parameters must be used together
Do not use both htmlContent and htmlUrl in the same template or campaign request as they are mutually exclusive
Never skip checking connection status with RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS before running workflows

faq.question: What is Rube MCP and why do I need it?
faq.answer: Rube MCP is a server that provides access to the Composio Brevo toolkit. It acts as a bridge between Claude and the Brevo API. You need it because this skill uses Rube's tools to interact with Brevo.

faq.question: How do I connect my Brevo account?
faq.answer: First ensure Rube MCP is connected, then call RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS with toolkit 'brevo'. If not active, follow the authentication link returned to complete OAuth connection with your Brevo account.

faq.question: Can I send email campaigns through this skill?
faq.answer: No. This skill can create, update, and schedule campaigns, but the final send must be triggered through the Brevo web interface. The API does not support direct campaign sending.

faq.question: Why do I get 'sender not verified' errors?
faq.answer: All sender email addresses must be verified in your Brevo account before use. Check verified senders with GET_ALL_SENDERS, and add new senders through the Brevo web interface under Sender settings.

faq.question: What is the difference between htmlContent and htmlUrl?
faq.answer: htmlContent contains the raw HTML for your email body, while htmlUrl points to a hosted HTML file. They are mutually exclusive - use one or the other, but not both in the same request.

faq.question: How do I handle large lists of campaigns or templates?
faq.answer: Use pagination with the limit and offset parameters. Default limit is 50 results. Increment offset by the limit value to retrieve additional pages. Check the count field in responses to determine total available items.
